title: Reusable sidebar chat surface
goal: Embed a compact AI chat in the left sidebar while preserving the full chat page.
requirements: Extract reusable chat markup/behavior where practical.
Use a dedicated sidebar ChatSession separated from ordinary full-page chat sessions.
Submit window_id, context_version and context_hint with messages and stream requests.
Keep a link to the full chat page.
Compact sidebar history by keeping the configured last N messages and summarizing older messages.
Use the shared ai.chat_settings runtime contract for both full_page and sidebar surfaces.
Resolve sidebar N from surfaces.sidebar.recent_message_limit with default 8.
Add get_chat_settings(surface) or equivalent helper instead of a separate sidebar_recent_message_limit setting.
Treat sidebar_summary as sensitive runtime state: no automatic memory ingestion and no raw audit/log payloads.
Store summary provenance with summarized message range/source message ids.
Keep window_id/context_snapshot_id/context_hint per sidebar message so multiple tabs do not erase context meaning.
Apply safety/masking pass before persisting sidebar_summary.
Do not add a sidebar session switcher in the MVP.
Do not add UI for viewing or regenerating sidebar_summary in the MVP.
acceptance: A message sent from sidebar appears in the dedicated sidebar session history.
The full chat page can open the sidebar session from the sidebar link.
The full chat page remains functional.
Streaming response works in sidebar mode.
Model selection is preserved or clearly scoped.
Sidebar panel pinned/collapsed state persists locally.
Compaction stores summary metadata and keeps the configured recent message tail.
Full-page and sidebar chat both read effective settings from the shared ai.chat_settings contract.
Summary metadata includes source message ids or from/to message range.
Summary is access-controlled like ChatSession and is not sent to memory automatically.
Default recent message tail is 8 messages.
Other chat sessions remain accessible from the full chat page, not through a sidebar switcher.
